selections.title: The Mahāsī Method
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive introduction to Mahāsī methodology with complete technical coverage and historical context.
selections.summary: Comprehensive introduction to Mahāsī Sayādaw's vipassanā method, covering noting technique and continuous awareness practice.

selections.title: Faith
selections.rationale: Most thorough exploration of saddhā through cognitive, emotional, volitional aspects with practical guidance for development.
selections.summary: Explores faith (saddhā) as spiritual faculty, distinguishing trust from belief through cognitive, emotional, and volitional aspects.

selections.title: The Buddha and the Hindrances
selections.rationale: Rich storytelling connecting Buddha's awakening journey to dependent origination and practical hindrance work with engaging narrative.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's awakening journey and practical guidance for working with hindrances through dependent origination understanding.

selections.title: Dullness, Lethargy, Restlessness and Pain
selections.rationale: Uses compelling Māra metaphor framework while providing detailed practical guidance for multiple challenging hindrance states.
selections.summary: Practical guidance on working skillfully with sloth/torpor, restlessness, and pain using Buddha's encounter with Māra as framework.

selections.title: Forgiveness
selections.rationale: Rich storytelling using Aṅgulimāla's transformation to demonstrate how paṭicca-samuppāda explains personal responsibility for suffering.
selections.summary: Uses Aṅgulimāla's story and paṭicca-samuppāda to show how we create our own suffering rather than being victims.

selections.title: Impermanence and Not-self
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Delves into anicca and anattā as universal characteristics that free us from ego-attachment and existential fear.

selections.title: Ethical Behaviour and the Awakening are One
selections.rationale: Thoroughly integrates ethics and wisdom while addressing common misconceptions about morality versus insight practice.
selections.summary: Explores the unity of wisdom (paññā) and virtue (sīla) in Buddhist practice, addressing the false separation between insight and ethical conduct.

selections.title: Wheel of Dependent Origination
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive and systematic presentation of twelve-link chain as practical psychological paradigm for present-moment experience.
selections.summary: Presents paṭicca samuppāda as a practical psychological paradigm for understanding how suffering arises moment by moment in present experience.

selections.title: Free Will
selections.rationale: Uniquely challenges Western individualism through Buddhist lens, revealing how control-seeking deepens suffering versus surrendering to dharma's natural unfolding.
selections.summary: Examines free will through Buddhist eyes, revealing how Western fixation on choice and control deepens suffering and misses liberation's true path.

selections.title: Kamma
selections.rationale: Foundational talk clearly explaining kamma through the five universal laws and dependent origination with strong theoretical framework.
selections.summary: Distinguishes personal intention (kamma) from external circumstances through the five universal laws governing existence and dependent origination.

selections.title: Rebirth
selections.rationale: Comprehensive exploration of Buddhist rebirth teachings including death process, six realms, and necessity for practice.
selections.summary: Explores Buddhist rebirth teachings, death process, six realms, and addresses whether belief in rebirth is necessary for Awakening.

selections.title: Premodern, Modern, Postmodernism and Buddhism
selections.rationale: Offers comprehensive framework for approaching Dhamma across different cultural mindsets with rich contextual exploration.
selections.summary: Explores how premodern, modern, and postmodern mindsets relate to Buddhist practice and the teacher-student relationship across historical contexts.

selections.title: Buddhist View of Christ's Teaching
selections.rationale: Unique interfaith perspective showing Buddhist approach to evaluating other spiritual traditions with personal depth.
selections.summary: Explores Christianity through Buddhist eyes, examining Jesus's teachings on detachment and love with deep personal interfaith insight.

selections.title: The Gratification, the Danger and the Escape
selections.rationale: Buddha's own framework for understanding Four Noble Truths through gratification-danger-escape; highly complete coverage.
selections.summary: Presents the Buddha's framework of gratification, danger, and escape for understanding how we seek pleasure, recognize its dangers, and find liberation.

selections.title: Bodhisatta's Birth to Renunciation
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive coverage of Buddha's early life with rich storytelling and unique spiritual reading framework.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's early life from birth to Great Renunciation, emphasizing spiritual reading and the arising of world-weariness (nibbidā).

selections.title: The Awakening
selections.rationale: Complete coverage of Buddha's awakening experience translated into practical Four Noble Truths framework.
selections.summary: Examines Buddha's awakening experience and its translation into core teachings of impermanence, suffering, and not-self through Four Noble Truths.

selections.title: The Years of Teaching and Parinibbāna
selections.rationale: Complete portrait of Buddha's humanity through vivid Pāli Canon stories spanning 45 teaching years to death.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's 45 teaching years and Parinibbāna, revealing his humanity, compassion, and practical wisdom through vivid Pāli Canon stories.

selections.title: Meditation and Daily Life
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive and practical guide connecting Vipassanā meditation with daily life activities.
selections.summary: Explores how Vipassanā meditation naturally extends into daily life through Noble Eightfold Path, transforming work into service and mindful awareness.

selections.title: Right Livelihood
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Explores sammā ājīva as fifth Eightfold Path factor, showing how work becomes a vehicle for spiritual awakening through mindful engagement.

selections.title: A Detailed Guided Meditation
selections.rationale: Complete step-by-step Mahāsī noting instruction from posture through insight into three characteristics.
selections.summary: Comprehensive Mahāsī noting meditation instruction from posture through breath awareness to insight into three characteristics.

selections.title: Investigative Eating Meditation — An Explanation
selections.rationale: Unique approach transforming mundane eating into liberation practice with rich storytelling and practical methods.
selections.summary: Uses eating as liberation practice to distinguish enjoying from indulging and develop freedom from attachment.

selections.title: The Hindrances: The Psychotherapy of Vipassanā
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive overview covering all five hindrances with complete practical guidance and clear categorization framework.
selections.summary: Examines five hindrances as Buddha's categorization of suffering with practical guidance for meditation obstacles.

selections.title: Right Awareness: Introduction to Vipassanā
selections.rationale: Comprehensive foundation linking Buddha's biography to Right Awareness practice with excellent storytelling and clear practical instruction.
selections.summary: Traces Buddha's spiritual journey from existential crisis to Awakening, laying foundations for Right Awareness understanding.

selections.title: Guided Mettā
selections.rationale: Most complete guided practice through all traditional mettā phases including forgiveness work and Karaṇīyamettā Sutta chanting.
selections.summary: Complete guided mettā practice through traditional phases, forgiveness work, and Karaṇīyamettā Sutta chanting.

selections.title: Exploring Dukkha
selections.rationale: Thorough exploration of dukkha's three levels with creative etymologies and mandala metaphor framework.
selections.summary: Examines dukkha as life's fundamental unsatisfactoriness and the transcendent path beyond suppression and indulgence.

selections.title: New Year Reflections
selections.rationale: Engaging practical guidance on four spiritual undertakings with memorable personal anecdotes and examples.
selections.summary: Four spiritual undertakings for New Year reflection: sufficiency, surrender, celibacy, and service as practical guidelines.

selections.title: The Body - Kāyagatāsati Sutta (MN 119)
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive exploration covering breath, postures, repulsiveness meditation, and death contemplation with exceptional practical detail.
selections.summary: Comprehensive exploration of body contemplation including breath awareness, postures, repulsiveness meditation, and death contemplation.

selections.title: Feeling (Vedanā)
selections.rationale: Most complete coverage tracing vedanā from initial contact through mental conditioning to liberation.
selections.summary: Exploration of vedanā aggregate, examining how feelings arise from sense contact and condition experience.

selections.title: The First Discourse: Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta - Turning the Wheel of the Dhamma
selections.rationale: Complete Buddha's first discourse with Four Noble Truths, rich storytelling about post-awakening teaching moment.
selections.summary: Buddha's first discourse at Sarnath, exploring Four Noble Truths and Eightfold Path after his awakening.

selections.title: Kamma, Rebirth and Transcendence: A Top-Down View of the Buddhadhamma
selections.rationale: Unique top-down perspective examining transcendence and rebirth from Buddhism's unconditioned viewpoint rather than materialism.
selections.summary: Top-down view of transcendence, kamma, and rebirth from Buddhism's unconditioned perspective rather than materialism.

selections.title: The Discourse to the Kālāmas: Discernment, the Ethical Life and Fake News
selections.rationale: Thoroughly covers Buddhist discernment principles with highly relevant modern applications to misinformation and critical thinking in our digital age.
selections.summary: Kālāma Sutta's teachings on discernment and evaluating truth, relevant for our age of misinformation.

selections.title: Buddha Day Address: What Was the Mindset of the Bodhisatta When He Left His Family and What Made Him Carry On?
selections.rationale: Deeply engaging existential exploration connecting Buddha's departure motivation to universal human experience of futility.
selections.summary: Existential despair that drove Bodhisatta to leave family and seek liberation from endless rebirth cycle.

selections.title: The Three Characteristics of Existence
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Three fundamental characteristics of existence: anicca, dukkha, and anattā as foundation of Buddhist understanding.

selections.title: Comments on a Statement by Mae Chee Kaew
selections.rationale: Most complete exploration of Mae Chee Kaew's liberation statement connecting Four Noble Truths to ending suffering.
selections.summary: Uses Mae Chee Kaew's liberation statement to explore Four Noble Truths and ending suffering.

selections.title: Importance of Resolution
selections.rationale: Thoroughly explores resolution through Buddhist psychology framework of five aggregates with practical New Year context.
selections.summary: Explores Buddhist psychology through five aggregates and the crucial role of resolution.

selections.title: How to Critique the Dhamma and Buddha
selections.rationale: Direct scriptural examination of Kālāma Sutta teaching on avoiding blind faith through investigation.
selections.summary: Examines the Kālāma Sutta's teaching on avoiding blind faith through direct experience over tradition or authority.

selections.title: Mindfulness: Time
selections.rationale: Uniquely frames Right Awareness as creating liberating space between original pain and reactive suffering responses.
selections.summary: Explores how Right Awareness creates space between original suffering and reactive responses, revealing liberation.

selections.title: Why Noting Without Stop
selections.rationale: Unique bee-flower metaphor explains continuous noting necessity with clear technical reasoning and vivid imagery.
selections.summary: Explains why continuous noting is essential in Mahasi practice using the bee-flower metaphor and vijñāna consciousness.

selections.title: Subliminal Inclinations, Defilements and Hindrances
selections.rationale: Systematic exploration of Buddha's threefold categorization offering unique theoretical depth missing from other talks.
selections.summary: Systematic exploration of Buddha's threefold categorization of unwholesome conditioning: anusaya, kilesa, and five hindrances.

selections.title: Dignity and the Qualities of a Ruler
selections.rationale: Unique approach linking personal dignity to spiritual practice through ten royal qualities framework.
selections.summary: Examines dignity as inner strength rooted in spiritual practice, drawing from ten royal qualities.

selections.title: Samatha-Vipassanā
selections.rationale: Rich storytelling traces Buddha's journey to illuminate samatha-vipassanā distinction with engaging historical detail.
selections.summary: Traces Buddha's spiritual journey to show development of samatha and vipassanā meditation approaches.

selections.title: The Mahāsī Method
selections.rationale: Explores foundational techniques with sports analogies and practical intention-noting guidance for daily application.
selections.summary: Explores foundational Mahāsī techniques: noting, slow movement, and catching intentions before they become actions.

selections.title: Samatha and Vipassanā
selections.rationale: Comprehensive exploration of concentration-insight relationship through Buddha's journey plus modern Mahasi method.
selections.summary: Bhante traces the Buddha's spiritual journey through samatha and vipassanā, showing how concentration and insight work together for awakening.

selections.title: The Problem Lies in the Looking
selections.rationale: Masterfully uses eyeglass metaphor to explain how fundamental delusion in perception creates suffering, making abstract concepts highly accessible.
selections.summary: Bhante explores how suffering stems from fundamental delusion in perception, using the analogy of wrong eyeglass prescription.

selections.title: There Is No Reason for Being!
selections.rationale: Uniquely engaging story of child's insight opens profound exploration of being versus becoming with joyful, accessible approach.
selections.summary: Drawing from a child's insight about existence, Bhante explores the difference between 'being' and 'becoming' in Buddhist understanding.

selections.title: Contemplation of Death (Again) - Living with Frugality
selections.rationale: Most engaging delivery with practitioner feedback, cemetery meditation guidance, and practical wisdom on frugal living.
selections.summary: Revisits death contemplation practice through practitioner feedback, exploring cemetery meditations and Buddhist approaches to frugal living during lockdown.

selections.title: Grief
selections.rationale: Rich personal examples and stories explore grief/attachment distinction with compassionate depth and practical wisdom.
selections.summary: Explores grief versus sorrow distinction, examining how attachment leads to suffering when losing loved ones.

selections.title: The Investigation of Eating
selections.rationale: Uniquely uses eating meditation as practical gateway to understanding right intention across all life experiences.
selections.summary: Uses eating meditation to distinguish between wholesome enjoyment and unwholesome indulgence in spiritual practice.

selections.title: The Discourse on Not-self (SN 22.59)
selections.rationale: Complete systematic examination of Buddha's second discourse, thoroughly deconstructing self through five aggregates with traditional progression.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's second discourse examining five aggregates, demonstrating how none can be considered as 'self'.

selections.title: Overcoming the Taints by Endurance
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive coverage of right effort through endurance, featuring Buddha's powerful saw simile with rich storytelling.
selections.summary: Explores overcoming mental taints through patient endurance, using Buddha's powerful simile of the saw.

selections.title: Overcoming Thīna-middha: Understanding Dullness and Lethargy
selections.rationale: Thorough exploration of thīna-middha with practical Buddha-based remedies and engaging contemporary examples throughout.
selections.summary: Explores thīna-middha hindrance of dullness and lethargy, offering practical Buddha-based advice for energetic awareness.

selections.title: Reflections on Polarization: A Dharma Perspective on Division and Understanding
selections.rationale: Contemporary application examining polarization through not-self lens, demonstrating dharma's relevance to modern social issues with practical solutions.
selections.summary: Examines modern polarization through Buddhist psychology, exploring how proliferation and identity create division and conflict.

selections.title: To Love or To Be Loved: That is the Question!
selections.rationale: Unique psychological exploration distinguishing loving to develop versus loving to be loved, with rich anecdotes and Buddhist psychology.
selections.summary: Explores crucial difference between loving to develop hearts versus loving to be loved, examining loneliness and self.

selections.title: Karanīya Mettā Sutta
selections.rationale: Complete systematic study of Buddhism's foundational loving-kindness discourse with thorough verse-by-verse analysis and traditional chanting.
selections.summary: Comprehensive study of the Buddha's foundational discourse on loving-kindness practice and its traditional verses.

selections.title: Skillful Discussion: The Buddha's Guide to Noble Conversation
selections.rationale: Most thorough exploration of skillful conversation with practical Buddha-based framework for polarized discussions.
selections.summary: Buddha's guidance on skillful conversation and meaningful dialogue, particularly relevant for polarized discussions.

selections.title: The Importance of Tranquillity — Passaddhi
selections.rationale: Most recent and refined treatment of tranquillity with practical physiological insights and applications.
selections.summary: Passaddhi as one of seven factors of Awakening, examining its effects on body-mind and meditation practice.

selections.title: Patient Forbearance
selections.rationale: Comprehensive exploration of khanti pāramī with rich examples covering abuse, adversity, and spiritual patience.
selections.summary: Khanti pāramī explored through Buddha's teachings on handling abuse, adversity, and reactivity with equanimity.

selections.title: Karuṇā: The Fourth Brahmavihāra - Compassion Practice and Pitfalls
selections.rationale: Exceptionally rich personal storytelling from 1970s Calcutta that powerfully illustrates compassion practice and its real-world challenges.
selections.summary: Karuṇā practice explored through personal stories from 1970s Calcutta, examining compassionate action and pitfalls.

selections.title: Renunciation — The Path of Letting Go
selections.rationale: Comprehensive coverage of renunciation as Noble Eightfold Path component with clear self-mortification versus skillful restraint distinction.
selections.summary: Nekkhamma as Noble Eightfold Path component, distinguishing skillful renunciation from harmful self-mortification through restraining craving.

selections.title: The Buddha as a Teacher
selections.rationale: Masterful demonstration of Buddha's teaching skills through complete Cūḷamāluṅkyasutta analysis with clear methodology breakdown.
selections.summary: Buddha's extraordinary teaching abilities demonstrated through Cūḷamāluṅkyasutta, showing skillful guidance away from speculative questions.

selections.title: Nibbāna: The Kevaddha Sutta and the Luminous Consciousness
selections.rationale: Comprehensive examination of Kevaddha Sutta's teaching on nibbāna as signless consciousness where elements find no footing.
selections.summary: Explores the Kevaddha Sutta's teaching on nibbāna as signless consciousness where the four great elements find no footing.

selections.title: The Nature of Nibbāna and the Path to Liberation
selections.rationale: Unique metaphor-rich approach using child development and Narcissus to illuminate consciousness without manifest objects.
selections.summary: Uses metaphors of child development and Narcissus to illuminate consciousness without manifest objects and liberation from self-deception.

selections.title: I own my actions. I inherit the results of my actions. I am born of my actions. I form relations through my actions. I live dependent on my actions. Whatever I do for good or for ill, I shall inherit the results. — Kamma and Rebirth
selections.rationale: Practical contemplative approach using the five reflections on kamma with guided meditation instructions.
selections.summary: Explores the five reflections on kamma, examining personal responsibility, consequences, and the interconnected nature of our actions.

selections.title: The Contemplation of Death
selections.rationale: Most complete exploration of maraṇasati as Buddha's most powerful practice, with excellent theoretical framework.
selections.summary: Explores maraṇasati as the Buddha's most powerful meditation practice for understanding impermanence and glimpsing nibbāna.

selections.title: The Eight Worldly Winds
selections.rationale: Complete systematic coverage of worldly conditions creating suffering with practical reflection questions for development.
selections.summary: Explores the eight worldly conditions that create mental turbulence and practical questions for developing equanimity toward them.

selections.title: Dukkha — The Buddha Grounds His Teaching on This Word
selections.rationale: Thorough foundational exploration of dukkha with etymology and three types of suffering.
selections.summary: Explores dukkha as Buddhism's foundational truth, from etymology through the Buddha's first discourse and three types of suffering.

selections.title: The Dangers of Indulgence
selections.rationale: Engaging Greek mythology weaves through middle path teaching, addressing pleasure-attachment cycles with practical examples.
selections.summary: Explores the middle path between sensual indulgence and self-mortification, examining how attachment to pleasure creates suffering cycles.

selections.title: Transcendent Dependent Arising: The Spiral Path from Dukkha to Liberation
selections.rationale: Unique focus on transcendent dependent arising showing how suffering transforms into liberation through practical entry points.
selections.summary: Explores the Upanisā Sutta's teaching showing seven practical entry points to transform suffering into the spiral path toward liberation.

selections.title: Identity and Not-Self
selections.rationale: Highly relatable examples of role identification make abstract teaching accessible, with practical meditation guidance and personal anecdotes.
selections.summary: Uses everyday examples of role identification to illustrate how anattā teaching addresses the suffering of over-identification.

selections.title: Right Livelihood
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Examines sammā ājīva as right lifestyle, covering traditional warnings and modern complexities of ethical work.

selections.title: Bāhiya of the Bark Cloth
selections.rationale: Thorough contextual analysis of the Buddha's most direct liberation teaching through six sense doors.
selections.summary: Explores the Udāna Sutta's direct liberation teaching through six sense doors, contextualizing Upanishadic background.

selections.title: Non-Violence vs. Pacifism
selections.rationale: Distinguishes Buddhist non-violence from philosophical pacifism with contemporary relevance and practical wisdom.
selections.summary: Distinguishes Buddhist non-violence as practical wisdom from philosophical pacifism, grounding ethics in experiential reality.

selections.title: Social Conditioning or Karma — Our Social Conditioning is a Given, but Even So We Have to Take Responsibility for It and Cleanse Any Unwholesomeness
selections.rationale: Addresses crucial distinction between personal karma and social conditioning with contemporary relevance and practical examples.
selections.summary: Examines difference between personal karma and social conditioning, exploring responsibility for purifying inherited cultural patterns.

selections.title: Buddha Day Address: His Training — The Fetters
selections.rationale: Unique combination of Buddha's extreme ascetic practices from Mahāsaccaka Sutta with practical ten fetters teaching.
selections.summary: Recounts Buddha's extreme ascetic practices from Mahāsaccaka Sutta and explores ten fetters binding beings to suffering.

selections.title: Paṭicca Samuppāda Revisited
selections.rationale: Advanced exploration showing how all other Buddhist teachings fork off from this central pillar of Buddha's dispensation.
selections.summary: Comprehensive exploration of twelve-link chain as both lifetime process and immediate psychological arising.

selections.title: Khandha, the Aggregates, as Person and Sati, Awareness
selections.rationale: Thorough exploration of khandhas as systematic deconstruction of personhood, integrating dukkha, awareness, and liberation with comprehensive coverage.
selections.summary: Explores dukkha and the five khandhas as systematic deconstruction of personhood, using awareness to examine psychophysical experience.

selections.title: Q&A: Emotions, Awareness/Delusion, Insight, Masculine/Feminine, Sense-of-Self, Porridge-itself-has-no-taste
selections.rationale: Included: only 2 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Explores emotions in meditation, awareness-delusion relationship, and Buddhist psychology through practical Q&A format.

selections.title: Aṅgulimāla: A Monologue
selections.rationale: Exceptional dramatic storytelling illustrating kamma and redemption through Aṅgulimāla's complete transformation from killer to awakened monk.
selections.summary: Dramatic first-person narrative of Aṅgulimāla's transformation from serial killer to awakened monk, exploring redemption and liberation.

selections.title: Setting the Wheel of the Dhamma in Motion
selections.rationale: Rich narrative of Buddha's teaching approach with cosmic imagery of Dhamma spreading through realms.
selections.summary: Examines Buddha's first formal teaching through Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta, establishing the wheel of Dhamma for liberation.

selections.title: Right Speech, Action and Livelihood
selections.rationale: Complete coverage of three ethical aspects of Eightfold Path with practical feedback loop explanation.
selections.summary: Explores three interconnected aspects of ethical conduct flowing from right understanding: speech, action, and livelihood.

selections.title: The Investigation of Eating
selections.rationale: Uniquely engaging with Buddha's striking desert metaphor, offering complete practical guidance for daily mindful eating meditation.
selections.summary: Explores mindful eating meditation using the Buddha's striking metaphor of eating one's son in the desert.

selections.title: 26 More Reflections
selections.rationale: Rich storytelling connecting truthfulness to post-truth world, politics, and personal relationships with engaging anecdotes.
selections.summary: Explores truth, interdependence, and sympathetic joy while concluding the series on values and perfections.

selections.title: 31b The Body
selections.rationale: Rich examination of Buddha's three-body teaching and skillful relationship development, featuring unique three-body framework perspective.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's teachings on establishing right relationship with body, examining Kāyagatāsati practice and transcending attachment.

selections.title: Discourse on the Establishment of Awareness: Satipaṭṭhāna Sutta (MN 10) - Elements and Charnel Ground Contemplations
selections.rationale: Excellent coverage of four elements and charnel ground contemplations with engaging storytelling and practical meditation guidance.
selections.summary: Explores four elements meditation and charnel ground contemplations, showing how to deconstruct sensations and contemplate mortality.

selections.title: Discourse on the Establishment of Awareness — Satipaṭṭhāna Sutta (MN 10)
selections.rationale: Essential Satipaṭṭhāna context showing vedanā within the complete mindfulness framework.
selections.summary: Continues Satipaṭṭhāna Sutta exposition, exploring foundations of feelings and mind states in mindfulness practice.

selections.title: Discourse on the Establishment of Awareness - Satipaṭṭhāna Sutta (MN 10)
selections.rationale: Directly covers the fourth foundation of satipatthana - mindfulness of dhammas - with comprehensive treatment of core teachings.
selections.summary: Explores fourth foundation of contemplating mind objects including five hindrances, aggregates, and sense bases.

selections.title: The Relay of Chariots — Rathavinīta Sutta (MN 24)
selections.rationale: Detailed exploration of seven purification stages leading to final nibbāna through structured dialogue format.
selections.summary: Explores seven stages of purification leading to final nibbāna through dialogue between Sāriputta and Puṇṇa Mantāniputta.

selections.title: The Dog Duty Ascetic Discourse — Kukkuravatika Sutta (MN 57)
selections.rationale: Engaging storytelling through unusual dog-duty ascetic encounters teaches four types of kamma and dangers of misguided spiritual practices.
selections.summary: Buddha's teaching on misguided ascetic practices and four types of kamma through unusual dog-duty ascetic encounters.

selections.title: Dhammapada 21-23: The Path of Diligence (Appamāda)
selections.rationale: Engaging narrative of Queen Samāvatī illustrates vigilance/diligence as path to deathless with accessible storytelling approach.
selections.summary: Explores appamāda (diligence/vigilance) as path to deathless through Queen Samāvatī and Magandiyā story.

selections.title: Dhammapada Chapter 3 - Verses 33 & 34: The Unruly Mind
selections.rationale: Rich storytelling with Thera Meghiya plus complete Vitakkasaṇṭhāna Sutta methods for mind training.
selections.summary: Examines mind training through Thera Meghiya's story and Buddha's Vitakkasaṇṭhāna Sutta practical methods.

selections.title: Dhammapada 256-257: Judgmental vs Judicious
selections.rationale: Unique angle distinguishing judgmental from judicious speech through corrupt judges story and dhammato principles.
selections.summary: Distinguishes between prejudicial thinking and wise discernment through corrupt judges story and dhammato principles.

selections.title: Dhammapada 46: How to View the Body
selections.rationale: Unique approach using foam/mirage metaphor with compelling bhikkhu story demonstrating body contemplation leading to liberation.
selections.summary: Body contemplation as foam and mirage, exploring impermanence and insubstantiality through bhikkhu's attainment story.

selections.title: Gratitude, Generosity and Renunciation
selections.rationale: Explores dāna as spiritual practice connecting gratitude, generosity, and renunciation in accessible narrative.
selections.summary: Explores dāna as spiritual perfection, distinguishing true generosity rooted in gratitude from ordinary giving with expectations of return.

selections.title: The Five Constant Contemplations
selections.rationale: Comprehensive coverage of all five contemplations from Aṅguttara Nikāya with complete doctrinal context and structure.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's five regular contemplations on aging, illness, death, separation, and karma from Aṅguttara Nikāya.

selections.title: The Discourse on Not-self — Anattalakkhana Sutta (SN 22.59)
selections.rationale: Comprehensive treatment of Anattalakkhana Sutta with clear methodical approach, examining control, permanence, and satisfaction across all khandhas.
selections.summary: Explores Buddha's second discourse examining how the five khandhas reveal illusion of self-control and permanent identity.

selections.title: The Dangers of Indulgence
selections.rationale: Most engaging storytelling with vivid Greek mythology examples while thoroughly covering indulgence versus enjoyment distinction.
selections.summary: Explores the Buddha's middle path between indulgence and self-mortification through vivid examples and practical guidance.

selections.title: Discourse to the Kālāmas
selections.rationale: Complete presentation of famous Kālāma Sutta with clear explanation of discerning truth through experience.
selections.summary: Bhante examines the Kālāma Sutta teaching how to discern truth through direct experience rather than authority.

selections.title: Desire the Root of Suffering
selections.rationale: Engaging dialogue format demonstrating Buddha's teaching method for Second Noble Truth understanding.
selections.summary: Bhante explores the Second Noble Truth through a dialogue demonstrating how desire based on wrong understanding causes suffering.

selections.title: Anusaya - Latent Tendencies
selections.rationale: Explores latent psychological tendencies underlying mental formations, offering unique perspective on deep conditioning patterns.
selections.summary: Bhante explores the seven anusaya - latent psychological tendencies that condition responses until eradicated through insight.

selections.title: Hetu — The Unwholesome Roots
selections.rationale: Systematically covers Buddhism's depth psychology through three unwholesome roots, providing essential foundation for understanding suffering's psychological origins.
selections.summary: Bhante explores the three unwholesome roots of greed, hatred and delusion as Buddhism's depth psychology.

selections.title: Paññā - Wisdom and Intuitive Intelligence
selections.rationale: Complements mindfulness coverage by exploring paññā as the intuitive intelligence underlying all awareness and insight.
selections.summary: Bhante explores paññā as the intuitive intelligence that underlies all awareness and insight in Buddhist practice.

selections.title: Sati — Knowing
selections.rationale: Distinctively explores sati as 'pure knowing' beyond thinking mind, offering unique perspective on unconditioned Buddha nature.
selections.summary: Presents sati as intelligent 'knowing' that liberates our unconditioned Buddha nature beyond thinking mind.

selections.title: Vīriya: Effort
selections.rationale: Unique angle on 'effortless effort' concept, distinguishing Buddhist approach from striving mentality with practical meditation guidance.
selections.summary: Explains 'effortless effort' in meditation as establishing objective observation rather than straining for results.

selections.title: Sacca: Honesty
selections.rationale: Focuses specifically on subtle dishonesty forms like exaggeration and self-deception in meditation practice.
selections.summary: Examines subtle forms of dishonesty including exaggeration and self-deception in meditation practice.

selections.title: Rūpa: Corporeality
selections.rationale: Distinctive analytical approach deconstructing direct body experience through four elements with accessible philosophical depth.
selections.summary: Understanding the body through direct experience of the four great elements rather than solid matter.

selections.title: Vedanā — Feeling
selections.rationale: Clearest systematic breakdown of five types of vedanā with practical meditation guidance.
selections.summary: Examining vedanā as the second aggregate, distinguishing physical and mental sensations in meditation.

selections.title: Saññā Perception & Vedanā Feeling
selections.rationale: Included: only 2 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: How saññā (perception) and vedanā (feeling) work together in every moment of awareness.

selections.title: Saṅkhāra: Volitional Conditioning
selections.rationale: Examines volitional conditioning as active realm of will, providing practical understanding of mental formations in meditation.
selections.summary: Saṅkhāra as the active realm of will where we engage with life through habits and intentions.

selections.title: 01. Sickness, Ageing and Death
selections.rationale: Distinctive approach through King Pasenadi dialogue, showing Buddha's unflinching directness about mortality's universal nature.
selections.summary: Examines the Buddha's unflinching teaching on mortality through dialogue with King Pasenadi, showing why facing life's harsh realities is essential.

selections.title: 02. Divine Messengers
selections.rationale: Unique angle through mythological framework of Buddha's awakening story, linking death awareness to karmic consequences.
selections.summary: Explores the three divine messengers—old age, sickness, and death—as profound reminders of our shared human condition and karmic consequences.

selections.title: The Two Darts
selections.rationale: Most comprehensive teaching on dukkha psychology using vivid parable, distinguishing inevitable from self-created suffering.
selections.summary: Illustrates how we compound unavoidable pain with mental reactions through the famous parable of two darts piercing the same person.

selections.title: The Eight Worldly Conditions
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Teaches how to navigate life's inevitable ups and downs through understanding the impermanent nature of worldly conditions.

selections.title: Detailed Sequence Leading to Conflict
selections.rationale: Engaging ice cream example tracing complete psychological sequence from pleasant feeling through craving to defensiveness and conflict.
selections.summary: Uses the relatable ice cream example to trace the psychological steps from pleasant feeling through craving to defensiveness and conflict.

selections.title: 09. Power
selections.rationale: Distinctive perspective contrasting unwholesome worldly power with spiritual power, connecting effort to interconnectedness and compassion.
selections.summary: Contrasts unwholesome power driven by greed, hatred, and delusion with the wholesome spiritual power of the four bases of psychic power.

selections.title: Use of Wealth for Happiness
selections.rationale: Included: only 3 talk(s) on this theme
selections.summary: Buddha's practical guidance to Anāthapiṇḍika on using righteously-earned wealth for personal happiness, family care, and community benefit.
